body:
Ákiko Í will add you to the list serve and Saturday zooms. Also I am going to share your email with my Doromind team.  Under no circumstance let your son come off of clozapine. Trends in neutrophil counts are meaningless and he doesn’t need to have the blood repeated early. If they insist on doing the bloodwork do it in the afternoon and have him exercise first. With this approach his neutrophil count will be increased. Dr L 
Sent from my iPhone

> On May 12, 2026, at 2:24 AM, Akiko Gaffney <akikogaffney@att.net> wrote:
> 
> ﻿
> ﻿
> 
> 
> Hi Dr. Laitman,
> 
> 
> 
> My name is Akiko Gaffney, and I live in the Sacramento area.
> 
> 
> 
> My 22-year-old son experienced his first CIP episode about four years ago. He recovered well initially, but due to addiction, he had a second CIP episode about a year later. We enrolled him in the PACE recovery program, and I am happy to say he has now been sober for 3 years and 3 months.
> 
> 
> 
> He has been taking Clozapine 300 mg for about a year and a half, along with Chantix. Last summer, he also completed 50 sessions of deep TMS through his psychiatrist, which was truly a game changer for him. He told us that his brain had felt “mushy” before, but afterward he felt much more connected and clear.
> 
> 
> 
> This past winter, we traveled to Japan for New Year’s (I am originally from Japan), and during the trip he began showing some symptoms again. We wondered whether the time change may have contributed, although we had checked with his psychiatrist beforehand and she felt the trip would be fine. He then received another 10 sessions of TMS in February, which seemed to help his symptoms significantly.
> 
> 
> 
> More recently, he developed TD symptoms on the left side of his mouth and eye. He has been treated with Ingrezza 60 mg, and thankfully the symptoms have improved nicely.
> 
> 
> 
> Overall, he is doing quite well. He will be transferring to a four-year college this fall. He currently lives in a sober living house, attends church and AA meetings regularly, and works part-time at a treatment center. We are very proud of him.
> 
> 
> 
> One thing we still notice is that he can be somewhat rigid with routines and self-care. For example, we often need to remind him multiple times about getting a haircut or eating more protein and maintaining good nutrition. He manages his grocery budget responsibly, which is wonderful, but we feel he still needs to eat a bit more.
> 
> 
> 
> Our biggest concern at the moment is continuity of psychiatric care. His psychiatrist has shifted her focus primarily to TMS treatment and is no longer providing medication management. Our son has recently been seeing a nurse practitioner instead, and my husband and I joined two Zoom appointments with him.
> 
> 
> 
> During one visit, the nurse practitioner briefly mentioned the possibility of changing him from Clozapine to another medication because he became irritated about needing another blood test only 2–3 weeks after his previous one. His recent labs showed WBC 3.1 and ANC 1566. She said those numbers were not alarming but wanted to continue monitoring them closely.
> 
> 
> 
> His psychiatrist mentioned that they are hoping to hire another medication-management psychiatrist by the fall. In the meantime, I was wondering whether you happen to know of a reliable psychiatrist in Orange County who has strong experience with Clozapine management and complex cases.
> 
> 
> 
> Thank you very much for your time. I truly appreciate any guidance you may have.
> 
> 
> 
> Kind regards,
> 
> 
> 
> Akiko Gaffney
> 
>

body:
Dear Clare and Brittany,

As you know we met with a new psychiatrist yesterday and the meeting was very positive and hopefully we now have a complete support team for Andrew. Dr. Jeevan Kumar, the Resident supporting Dr. Ginger Shupe, spoke with Andrew for about an hour and then Dr. Shupe came in and spoke with Andrew for another 45 minutes or so. They were both very warm and friendly, which was great for Andrew and for me too. Dr. Shupe, the Medical Director of Outpatient Psychiatry at Larking Community Hospital, appeared to be very receptive to partnering with Doro Mind in support of Andrew. 

In order to arrange for the phone call or zoom with Dr. Shupe please send an e-mail to: outreach@larkinhospital.com attention Dr. Kumar and he will coordinate a time for the call with Dr. Shupe. They also had Andrew sign a consent form for you to share medical information with them. I have attached a copy below.

I also wanted you to have a list of the medications Andrew is taking so that we are all on the same sheet of music:

-  400MG Clozapine at night and 25MG in the morning
-  80MG of Propranolol ER
-  100 MG of Metformin ER
-  100MG of Alapurinol (his GP prescribed this because his uric acid was so high and his father get gout 

Supplements prescribed by his GP Dr. Stephanie Duncan Garcia:

-  Icosapent Ethyl 1 gram each capsule. He takes two capsules twice a day (his GP prescribed this because his LDL is very high
-  B-12 Folate prescribed by his GP
-  D3
-  Magnesium Glycinate

His blood pressure figures over the last couple of days have been: 9 May/101/74/96BPM, 10 May/104/72/91BPM, 11 May/102/71/101BPM, 12 May/111/76/96BPM, 13 May 104/63/99BPM

Andrew is scheduled to see Dr. Duncan-Garcia for a blood draw on 18 May to determine his serum level. When I get back from my trip (I’ll be gone 16-23 May) I’ll provide the results to you.

Thanks for all you do and if you have any questions please let me know.

Gale

PS Andrew is cc’d on this note :-)

>> Hi Susie,
>> 
>> It was great connecting with you yesterday! Brady really enjoyed the welcome call.
>> 
>> As promised, I am sending the Compass proposal on Brady’s behalf. 
>> 
>> What’s Included in Doro Compass:
>> Structured support to build stability and reduce setbacks
>> Expert guidance on medication management, work with a nurse practitioner overseen by Dr. Robert Laitman, including review of therapeutic drug monitoring to optimize results and reduce side effects
>> Initial 45-minute session with our psychiatric nurse practitioner to review current needs and develop a care plan
>> Follow-up sessions, typically two in the first month, and one or two in the following months as needed
>> Note that direct prescribing is not available under this plan
>> Consultations with outside providers to coordinate care and make recommendations
>> Personalized research and care navigation to help you explore treatment options
>> On-demand messaging with your Doro Guide or nurse practitioner between check-ins
>> Family education and coaching through weekly groups, online resources and individual sessions
>> 
>> Pricing Details:
>> Doro Compass offers two flexible tiers of monthly support:
>> 
>> Tier 2 ($2,100/month): All members begin here with the highest level of support to help reduce setbacks and build stability.
>> Tier 1 ($700/month): As you or your loved one make progress, our clinical team will recommend when it's appropriate to move to this lower-intensity tier.
>> 
>> Progression from Tier 2 to Tier 1 is based on recovery progress. While many families remain in Tier 2 for several months, our goal is to support eventual transition. We offer income-based financial support. To verify your eligibility, please share your household size and income.
>> Next Steps:
>> When you’re ready to move forward, go to our membership portal <https://go.doromind.com/purchase> (guide here <https://www.doromind.com/member-portal-guide>). Note that from time to time we may have waitlist only <http://doromind.com/waitlist> availability which you will see on the site. Once we initiate your membership we will then begin the enrollment process to match you with your care team within 3 business days. If you have further questions to discuss, schedule a call <https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call> anytime.
>> 
>> 
>>

body:
Hi Rich,

Thank you for the update. I know it’s a stressful time with complex
transitions and I’m sorry to hear your sister is not in great shape at the
moment. Our team can support you through the transition. The way we would
approach that is through the Doro Compass plan. This way we can set up a
team of support and consult on medication, care transitions and advocacy.
This kind of transition and plan creation is something that our team is
quite familiar with. We don’t consider it a ‘one-off basis’ though because
it typically takes time to coordinate. The membership is month-to-month
though, so there is some flexibility if your needs change.

If you came to NY we would be able to provide direct treatment through the
Doro Care services. I have attached a description of that membership, so
that you can review. It is often in the case that if we move from Doro
Compass to Doro Care, you can continue working with the same team.

I know it’s a lot to think through. Please feel free to book a call
<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call> with our team if you want to talk
through the options.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

 https://qr.doromind.com/

Hi Nima and Shamik,

It was great meeting with you both today. Below are the details for your
upcoming session with John and how to share the items we discussed today.
Please share what you can before Friday, but don’t worry if you can’t get
to everything. We'll provide guidance and support based on the information
you have available.
You can email these items to me at *kendra@doromind.com
<kendra@doromind.com>* (please CC: *guides@doromind.com
<guides@doromind.com>*), upload them via the app or fax them to: (866)
202-6631.

*Nima:*

   -

   Recent Blood Work: Please send screenshots of Aviya’s labs from the last
   two weeks, specifically including the clozapine serum level.

*Shamik:*

   -

   Psychiatric Treatment Records: Any pertinent historical records
   detailing Aviya’s psychiatric treatment and medication history.
   -

   Documentation: A copy of the Letter of Conservatorship and a copy of her
   Medi-Cal insurance card, if possible.

*Meeting information*:
Intro NP Visit-Nima, Shamik with John
Friday, May 15 · 9:00am –9:45am
Google Meet joining info
Video call link: https://meet.google.com/igc-yejf-gqz
Or dial: ‪(US) +1 442-274-1216‬ PIN: ‪211 330 723‬#

Please let me know if you have any questions at all before your meeting on
Friday. Thanks so much and I will speak to you soon!

Warmly,

Kendra Wilson
Doro Mind Guide

Hi all!

I wanted to pull this group together on a single thread, just to make sure we all had a shared picture of the current state of the agreements.

Dr Feldman told us that he thought he was waiting on a new version of the agreements. I talked with Alexis today. She and I think the versions you have are up-to-date. Dr Feldman and Nick, if you're expecting a new revision, it would be great to know that (as well as any specifics you're looking for).

We're having a conversation with Dr Feldman a little later today to try to push the substance of the conversation forward, so it would be lovely to do that with the clearest shared understanding of where we were from a legal/administrative point of view.

Thanks!

Brady

Hi Jessica,

Thank you for taking the time to connect with me today. As I mentioned, we
have membership available starting *Monday May 18th* and we are ready to
begin enrollment if you would like to proceed. Please reply within 72hrs to
confirm your intention to enroll. As described in our waitlist policy
<https://www.doromind.com/waitlist> if we don't hear back in that timeframe
we will need to move to the next member on our list.

When we receive your written confirmation we will charge the card on file
for the first month of membership ($1,080/mo for Doro Compass with
financial assistance) or we can invoice you if you prefer. This will
initiate your membership and begin our onboarding process with your Doro
Mind care team. Note that this does not mean we will have the first meeting
the same day, only that our team begins work.

Please let us know if you have any questions. Thank you!

~Stephanie

1. We can submit RN and APRN license applications simultaneously in CT.  
2. Unfortunately this isn't the case in NJ; we need to submit RN, then APRN
3. Licensing in MA is "notoriously slow especially for prescribers"
So, I recommend we:
a. submit applications for CT RN, CT APRN, NJ RN this week,
b. forgo a provisional license in CT (though I'm still awaiting some details to confirm timing and cost which I expect tomorrow) and
c. revisit (next week) internal decisions about when/ for whom to consider licensing in additional states especially MA and CA

Brady if you're good with this, then Keren would love for you to review updated applications (all should be updated through tasks in Assured's platform based on the Gdocs that you've shared with Magdiel) to make sure that they've corrected everything.  Do we also want a final final review by our NPs too?

Still Using AI Like a Chat? Why 95% of Founders Are Stuck at Level 1 (and  
don't even know it)
Tuesday Jun 2, 2026 ⋅ 11am – 12pm
Eastern Time - New York

Location
https://vibelowcode.webinargeek.com/still-using-ai-like-a-chat?cst=calendar-invites	
https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Fvibelowcode.webinargeek.com%2Fstill-using-ai-like-a-chat%3Fcst%3Dcalendar-invites&sa=D&source=calendar&ust=1779126540000000&usg=AOvVaw3e7pIaAowo-dAY6a8OiqYz

Only if you are seeing this within 5 mins to start the webinar, click here  
to join:  
https://vibelowcode.webinargeek.com/still-using-ai-like-a-chat?cst=calendar-invites

Otherwise, to register:
'Yes' or 'Maybe' to save your spot and receive reminders.
'No' if you're not interested, and I won't follow up.

You're probably wondering why you're receiving this invite out of the blue.

This is for founders, operators, marketers, and agency owners who already  
use AI every day, and quietly suspect they're barely scratching the surface.

Quick diagnostic. Be honest:

Are you still using AI like a glorified chat?
Do you re-explain the same context every single time you ask it something?
Does it still hallucinate or give you confident-but-wrong answers?
Do you know how agents and sub-agents work, and how to use them efficiently  
on tokens and output quality?
Do you know the difference between probabilistic AI calls and  
deterministic, rule-based logic, and when to use each?
Do you understand why a single prompt is never 100% reliable for anything  
production grade?

If 3 or more of those land, you're still at level 0 or 1. That's where 95%  
of the founders we talk to actually live. And it's why their AI workflow  
ships toys, never real businesses.

In this free 60-minute masterclass, we run the Kreante Lead Architect  
framework on you:

The 5 levels of AI maturity, and the cliff between level 3 and level 4.
Why level 1 AI is perfectly fine for prototypes and vibe coding, and lethal  
for production grade systems (secure, scalable, robust).
How agents, sub-agents, deterministic guardrails, and token discipline move  
you from level 1 to level 5.
The 4 things AI alone will never give you: architecture, security, senior  
judgment, domain.
Real $1.8M case study: pre-revenue founder, AI native MVP shipped in 8  
weeks, funded 6 months later.

The goal isn't to shame anyone for chatting with AI. It's to show what's  
actually possible the moment you stop treating it that way.

Limited spots.

Organizer
Jorge Del Carpio
jorge@vibecodingwebinar.com

Reply for brady@doromind.com and view more details  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=c2YyMmNycGEzc2hwcDUzOGE4OXZqaWhtdTggYnJhZHlAZG9yb21pbmQuY29t&tok=Mjcjam9yZ2VAdmliZWNvZGluZ3dlYmluYXIuY29tYTg3ZWZhYzc5ODI3NWE2MzhiNGFiZmRiOWRjZjg4ZmFkNjAxNTM3Mg&ctz=America%2FNew_York&hl=en&es=1
Your attendance is optional.

~~//~~
Invitation from Google Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/

You are receiving this email because you are subscribed to calendar  
notifications. To stop receiving these emails, go to  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/r/settings, select this calendar, and  
change "Other notifications".

Forwarding this invitation could allow any recipient to send a response to  
the organizer, be added to the guest list, invite others regardless of  
their own invitation status, or modify your RSVP.

Learn more https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/37135#forwarding

Slack
 
 

 

 

 
 

  

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufGnMMjByLrWPXZy1evEVzQbQYwhO0YEuMDN62BgQ8ho8LxxK0rpliPGNXN-4oZ4WcG_B1eBAr5NWpfpw9fdxgTd2APdOhCNQY 

May • 2026
 

Slack Admin Updates
 

Read on to prepare yourselves and your members for new capabilities, changes and deprecations.
 

WHAT'S NEW

  

Add Agent to Slack

Developers can now bring agents built on third-party platforms directly into Slack. Slack will handle the Slack-specific integration and app creation, while developers will retain full control of their agent infrastructure. Agents will appear as native Slack apps to end users.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufHxbWMUfw1liYDKUgYRh29g9HHandE-UgVbdJ7xqm8sDW7FcW1v4FL5bHa-JFg6fXP0XWY3I-mOvUVH_wqeAdOwuAQedZ127L1ikDz 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Require email verification for all new member invites

When an Enterprise admin invites a non-SSO user to their workspace via email, the invitee will always need to verify that they have email access by entering a short-code. This ensures the invitee has email access since account creation can be shared to someone who does not have email access.

  

Expand flag content to channel names, topics & descriptions (Enterprise plans)

Flag content allows users to report potentially harmful content to their admins for potential removal. This month, the scope of flaggable content is expanding from messages and profile content to channel names as well as topics & descriptions that appear on channels, MPDMs and DMs. Customers who already have flag content enabled for messages will automatically have it enabled for channel names, topics & descriptions.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufINGqmO-ffQRed6H5EyLjJnqiY3jnGA47h2oICNIsELWMFG31aOt1w9d2w3K4jSYbqRmQ5gj7XgD7hby-aBoEPTVyoCRdyy1FdAee- 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Multi-domain support for GitHub data source in enterprise search

Enterprise search now supports connecting multiple GitHub accounts within a single GitHub connector, allowing users to search across multiple GitHub orgs or domains without setting up separate connectors.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufJ_BXQIOFCbMAokM0POdRc7GMjKx3ZLBm5-FhyFhp3OWsuoU9dun5K1QAPwpmSx0ZuKRERGQLs4ICYlr9aach71jq62ZRuc_UqqIPj 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Slackbot skills (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

Skills are reusable instructions stored as canvases that teach Slackbot how to complete a specific task, saving users from having to repeat themselves every time. Also available to Business+ v1 and Enterprise Grid teams with the Slack AI add-on.

  

Slackbot native Slack actions (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

Slackbot can now help take actions in Slack, including sending messages, creating or archiving channels, updating your sidebar, and setting your status or do not disturb. Slack actions are taken according to a user’s existing permissions. No action is taken automatically.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufKhOp5tO5kLB2XOujEZAPbwflEilfgYhlxSy1nI5Rck3qSKguqWQ1QfATCVXbLcM70uZbtyER04rvEymX9Ty8B7QvGsw8-v3oGevo8 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Slackbot write to calendar and email (Enterprise+ plans)

Slackbot can now help draft and send emails or create calendar invites directly from a conversation. Both write actions require explicit user review and approval before Slackbot proceeds. Also available to Enterprise Grid teams with the Slack AI add-on.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufLE5-WM5gfr8RZOAUPH0wxUxGHmOZ1lkbb5jWCgMsXYobFQh0uTAuXQHJtE0G_ZJ2dzCprZLB503maTsIaN-n02KspS87-8XZwZWep 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Slackbot visual context (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

When Slackbot is open in split view, it can automatically pick up on what you have open: a channel, thread, canvas, list, or Salesforce record, and use that context to give more relevant responses. Also available to Business+ v1 and Enterprise Grid with the Slack AI add-on.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufMiiksGKFYy-K6ICleey6RmWZ_WuA4FCmAQjl05WkCmV4pitiL0tbAKegY0eOhz527i3Ugigo1r9wUdAMjCDtQrSVYEytB5uBzyJWk 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Schedule recurring Slackbot prompts (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

Members can now ask Slackbot to run a prompt on a recurring schedule, like a weekly summary or a daily check-in, without needing to build a workflow.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufN5njxprbcKyF6k6sWVjJwyNgxI2CYpl63Xr6OwSzu9r2cujxtjtYMEJjL_lutSrj6WPg7EM1b9f7PUBInZzwgJLU5ath2h4xkkiAK 
Learn more ‌ →

  

AI generate step in Workflow Builder (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

Workflow Builder now includes an AI Generate step. Choose a task (like summarizing a message, translating text, or drafting a response), set up your prompt, and the step will automatically produce the output when the workflow runs. Admins can control availability via org settings. Also available to Business+ v1 and Enterprise Grid with the Slack AI add-on.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufOh6pX_D-TL6ko0WRNreONjt0RTvnv36Br65Nb4XLPgi-1rIk0ABiHWIBDbvLP5HBWcehbPVGl0XU5kWlitDUa5wzgkvg8pKxh1oNj 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Developer tools for bringing external agents into Slack (Slack Agent Kit)

We have recently released the Agent Kit, a set of developer tools, including enhanced Bolt framework capabilities, new CLI commands, and sample apps, that make it faster to bring coded AI agents built on any platform into Slack.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufPWFgWbveWiTnRU3zUERhQ2KVKZSggjve_OP77Pc6LdRqadjYF4ek5pmvmsUFfUsz5varT8cnWJxbz63Wc28nYVtuvjytOKPCO8_n- 
Learn more ‌ →

COMING SOON*
 

  

Salesforce channels default-on for new Enterprise Edition+ orgs

New Salesforce orgs on Enterprise or Unlimited Edition will automatically get a connected Slack workspace and Salesforce Channels enabled on Sales and Service Cloud record pages. Existing orgs will be able to enable the new Slack button directly on their record pages with a single admin toggle.

  

Private Salesforce channels

Salesforce channels will gain a new "Private" visibility mode, giving admins and channel managers a true private channel option beyond the existing "Limited access" setting.

  

Custom file sections

Users will be able to create named sections in the Files tab sidebar to organize their files (canvases, lists, docs, etc.), similar to how channel sections work in the sidebar.

  

Slackbot deep research (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

Slackbot deep research is a feature that enables Slackbot to conduct multi-source investigations on complex questions, going beyond simple information retrieval. Also available to Business+ v1 and Enterprise Grid with the Slack AI add-on.

  

Slackbot charts (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

Slackbot will soon be able to render data visualizations directly in the conversation, either when asked or it may generate one proactively when relevant. Also available to Business+ v1 and Enterprise Grid with the Slack AI add-on.

  

Slackbot list creation and management (Enterprise+ and Business+ v2)

Members will be able to create and manage Slack Lists through a conversation with Slackbot. They can describe the list they need, add and update columns, and add items, without needing to navigate through menus. New lists created this way are private by default.

  

Today view: Beta

Admins will be able to opt into a beta program for a new AI-powered homepage that automatically groups and summarizes important unreads, identifies action items, and reminds users ahead of meetings

  

Focus mode

Members will be able to turn on focus mode to pause push notifications and reduce visual noise in the sidebar including bolding, badging, and sidebar intensity.

  

Organization level user groups.

Org Owners and Admins will be able to make IDP groups and Admin groups visible in Slack so that they can be used for mentions and channel invites.

  

Upcoming deprecations

  

OS, app, and browser versions reaching end of support on May 18, 2026

In accordance with our support lifecycle policy, we have discontinued support for a number of operating systems, app versions, and browsers.

https://click.email.slackhq.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzV9AAEAAAAAAFs4AufQweFLOkrsrOd5HdoofxdZsPZ35Rlu44zeXcoUJLdm2OWeTM5UL9l70qtnvn6QUfVxM00pqeRV6ajZk-I_bRPxuM8bZslBVxEXep_Y 
Learn more ‌ →

Deprecation of .exe installer for Windows in March 2027

Slack will be discontinuing support for the legacy Squirrel-based Windows .exe installer. After March 2027, the downloadable .exe file will no longer be available on our download page, and there will be no new releases of this installer package. Your IT team should transition to deploying the MSIX package, which offers enhanced enterprise-level features and modern auto-updating capabilities. Important: Existing Slack installations will continue to function and auto-update normally. This change only affects new deployments.

body:
Hi Wendy,

It was great catching up with you last Wednesday! I wanted to follow-up on
our call and confirm that we've updated your Doro Compass membership for
Max from Tier 2 to Tier 1. You should've seen a charge to your card on-file
for $700 yesterday. This will be your new monthly membership rate for Tier
1.

I've attached a document that summarizes the differences between these 2
tiers for your reference. Here's also the change in frequency of visits we
discussed last week:

   - *NP visits Max with John*: Reduce frequency from weekly to 2x a month
   starting in May, then shift to once a month starting in mid-June
   - *Recovery Coaching Max with Heather)*: Will start 2–3 sessions/month
   in May with a focus of working on goals beyond medication/treatment.
   Heather will work with John & Max to assess frequency in June.
   - *Family Coaching Wendy/Joel with Brittany*: A finite series of
   sessions for Wendy & Joel to further explore & develop communication skills
   with Max.

Here's what will remain unchanged:

   - On-going access to Doro Mind Community chat boards and team chats
   - Weekly Caregiver Connections meetings
   - Access to our AI practice feature
      - evidence based approaches for caregivers and family to practice
      communication approaches with a loved one in recovery

In terms of next steps,

   - *Max/Heather* - We're in contact with Max to schedule his intro
   meeting with Heather, and
   - *You/Joel & Brittany* - Brittany reached out via your personal chat on
   Monday to schedule time for you and Joel. Feel free to use this email
   thread to coordinate & setup that meeting time too.
   - *Aaron's membership* - Reach out to guides@doromind.com when and if
   you want to also explore membership for Aaron. There's an option to
   complete a financial aid application should be interested as well.

We're so excited for Max and look forward to our continual teamwork towards
his recovery!

Sincerely,
Minna

-- 
Minna C
Doro Guide

*Doro Mind Inc.* <https://www.doromind.com/>
*Rebuild & Find Hope*

** Register today for NAMI’s Upcoming Webinar:
------------------------------------------------------------
https://nami-org.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_4c8q0wsvQ_y3p8unFY8nIQ

** NAMI Presents:
------------------------------------------------------------

** NAMI Webinar: Navigating Schizophrenia in Black & Hispanic Communities (Rompiendo el Silencio)
------------------------------------------------------------

** Tuesday, May 19, 2026 | 3:00 – 4:00 p.m. ET
------------------------------------------------------------

In recognition of World Schizophrenia Awareness Day, observed annually on May 24, this session explores lived experiences, storytelling, and reducing stigma in BIPOC communities impacted by schizophrenia. Maria Case (https://www.linkedin.com/in/maria-case-414975339/) , founder of Kompashion, reflects on her family’s experience navigating a parent’s schizophrenia and the importance of storytelling and open conversation within Spanish-speaking communities. 

Ray Lay (https://www.nami.org/who-we-are/meet-the-board/ray-charles-lay/) , a NAMI board member and national advocate, shares his personal journey with schizophrenia, homelessness, and incarceration. He explains how these lived experiences influence his efforts in peer support, criminal justice reform, and policy advocacy.

Together, Maria and Ray offer powerful perspectives on support, healing, dignity, and access to care. Their stories help humanize schizophrenia, challenge stigma, and elevate voices too often excluded from the mental health narrative.
REGISTER NOW (https://nami-org.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_4c8q0wsvQ_y3p8unFY8nIQ)

This webinar is hosted by NAMI’s Community Health Equity Alliance (CHEA) (http://nami.org/chea) . CHEA envisions a future in which Black/African ancestry and other minoritized communities can easily access culturally responsive mental health support. It empowers communities and individuals with mental health conditions to connect with equitable mental health support through education, partnership, and community-centered solutions. Guided by E²C² (Educating, Expanding, Convening, and Connecting), CHEA’s  goals are to provide greater clarity on where to go, what steps to take, and who to contact for help. For more, please visit nami.org/chea or email chea@nami.org (mailto:chea@nami.org?subject=&body=) .

Reserve your spot today (https://nami-org.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_4c8q0wsvQ_y3p8unFY8nIQ)  and submit your questions for our guest experts through the registration form!

body:
Hi Brady,

We look forward to seeing you on Saturday at the NAMIWalks Westchester event.
Our Community Resource partners are an important part of our event.

Details as follows:

The walk will take place on Saturday, May 16, 2026, rain or shine.

 Location is Ridge Road Park, 287 Ridge Road, Hartsdale, NY, 10530, approx. one mile from Taconic/Sprain Parkway. Free parking.

•     Bring your own table and chair
•     Please bring your own tablecloth, materials and giveaways.
•     Arrival time is 8:00 a.m. As you walk through the balloon arch, please go to the tent marked Sponsors & Speakers Check-In to receive a thank you bag       and you will be directed to the area in which you can set up your table. Electricity and Wi-Fi will not be available.
•     Walker check-in will begin at 8:30 a.m., at which time, attendees will have time to get refreshments and visit tables prior to the opening ceremony and                 warm-up at 9:30; walk will start approx. 10:00. Walkers will return to the same area for table visits and activities.
•     Please do not leave your table until at least 11:30 a.m. and please take with you any leftover materials, or feel free to dispose of them in a recycling or                 trash receptacle.
•     Please share on social media that you are supporting our walk and tag us as follows:

Facebook @NAMIWestchester.org
Twitter @namiwestwalk
Instagram @namiwestchester
LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/nami-westchester-inc/

• Contact info:
Before walk day, from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m., call our office at (914) 592-5458.
Emergency number on walk day, Marie Considine at (914) 258-7613.

Many thanks! See you on Saturday!

Cindy & Leslie Pollak

Cindy Fasolino

Business Operations Administrator/ Spanish Liaison

NAMI Westchester, Inc.

220 White Plains Road, Suite 125, Tarrytown, NY 10591

Helpline/Office: (914) 592-5458

Email: Cindyf@namiwestchester.org

We Support - Educate - Advocate

www.namiwestchester.org<http://www.namiwestchester.org/>

Hi Carolyn,

I am writing to confirm that you have successfully purchased a spot on our
waitlist. We are glad to have you with us and look forward to supporting
you and your family.

Doro Mind provides expert, recovery-oriented care for individuals
navigating psychosis and their families. Our services are overseen by our
Medical Director, Dr. Robert Laitman, a world-recognized expert in
clozapine management and the treatment of schizophrenia. Depending on your
needs, we offer two primary programs:

   -

   *Doro Care:* Direct clinical treatment and medication management
   (currently available in the Greater New York area: NY, NJ, and CT).
   -

   *Doro Compass:* A national program providing expert medication guidance,
   family coaching, and consultation with your existing outside providers.

To help us move forward, *I highly encourage *you to complete our short intake
form <https://go.doromind.com/request-appointment>. This will help us
better understand your specific situation and provide more tailored
recommendations:

You are also very welcome to book a brief welcome call
<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call> with our team to discuss your goals
and answer any questions.

We look forward to connecting with you soon.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

Hi Tom,

This is Pao from the Enrollment Team.

Thank you for reaching out to us. We received your email inquiry and would
like to offer our support to you and Ethan. At Doro Mind, we specialize in
helping families and individuals navigating psychosis, particularly through
the expert use of clozapine and comprehensive side-effect management.

We offer two programs:

   - Doro Care (available in NY, NJ, CT): direct psychiatric and
   therapeutic treatment
   - Doro Compass (available nationwide): expert guidance and coaching for
   families, medication support, and collaboration with outside providers.

To help us better understand your specific situation and provide tailored
recommendations, we invite you to complete our short intake form
<https://go.doromind.com/referral>.

If you would like to speak with our team directly, schedule a welcome call
<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call>.

We look forward to connecting and helping you navigate the next steps
toward recovery.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Wednesday, May 13, 2026
*Ken's Daily Briefing*
AI & Schizophrenia Research Digest
Today's news highlights the shift from digital abstraction to physical reality and the growing "oversight tax" in both engineering and medicine. In AI, we are seeing the rise of Physical AI and robotics foundation models alongside a sobering report that AI is actually increasing developer cognitive load. In psychiatry, the launch of Phase 3 trials for LB-102 targets the "last mile" of schizophrenia treatment—cognitive dysfunction—while the WHO launches a global initiative to close the 20-year mortality gap. For Doro Mind, the implication is clear: better tools only work if we improve the triage and oversight systems that deploy them.
*Artificial Intelligence*
<https://etfdb.com/|The Rise of Physical AI & Robotics>
ETF DB
2026 is becoming the year of "integration." Companies like Skild AI and Physical Intelligence are licensing robot foundation models—AI "brains" for motor control—to hardware firms, moving Physical AI from lab prototypes to mass commercial deployment.
<https://harness.io/|The AI Productivity Paradox>
Harness
A major report from Harness (The State of Engineering Excellence 2026) finds that while leadership sees productivity gains, developers are spending 30% more time on manual code reviews and fixing AI-generated bugs. The "cognitive load" of managing AI output is a growing burnout risk.
<https://sherlocq.ai/|Sherlocq Launch: Regulatory AI for Finance>
Sherlocq
A new AI-native regulatory intelligence platform launched today, covering 30+ jurisdictions. It focuses on "verifiable compliance" and auditable reasoning, a direct response to the hallucination risks of generic models in finance.
<https://googlecloudpresscorner.com/|Albertsons & Google Cloud: Computer Vision in Supply Chain>
Google Cloud
The grocery giant is deploying an Intelligent Quality Control tool powered by Gemini to inspect fresh produce via computer vision at distribution centers—a tangible example of AI moving into the physical supply chain.
*Schizophrenia Research*
<https://www.biospace.com/|LB-102 Enters Phase 3 for Cognitive Symptoms>
BioSpace
LB Pharmaceuticals announced the NOVA-2 trial for LB-102 (N-methyl amisulpride). Post-hoc analysis confirms direct improvements in memory and executive function—symptoms that standard antipsychotics typically fail to touch.
<https://who.int/|WHO Launches Global Mortality Initiative>
WHO
The World Health Organization is moving to bridge the 15–20 year life expectancy gap for people with schizophrenia by mandating the integration of cardiovascular and metabolic screenings into routine psychiatric care.
<https://www.swissinfo.ch/|Cerebellum Circuits & Negative Symptoms>
SwissInfo
Researchers in Geneva have identified a specific circuit in the cerebellum linked to listlessness and social withdrawal. A new clinical trial is testing non-invasive magnetic pulse stimulation to "reset" these circuits.

body:
CS - 5/13/2026
Coach - mala

9am - woke up. Had water with lemon.

10–11am we did a workout on YouTube. Reported she feels like she may be
ready to return to yoga classes within the next couple of days.

Went over schedule for today. I asked if her adviser had replied to her
email yet and she said no.I showed her a computer shortcut I discovered by
accident on my keyboard. She became interested in it and then started
looking up more shortcuts on Youtube.  She wrote down the different
commands and shortcuts for both of us to refer back to.

1:30pm meds.

2:30–4pm met with her mum to look at furniture and design ideas for the
house. Mood  overall were good.

Although, while C was at the counter paying for something.  her mum shared
privately with me that a friend had mistaken their dinner plans for next
week when they were actually scheduled for tomorrow and she was going to be
annoyed if her friend cancels.  Her mum commented that she finds C
exhausting and does not always like being around her too much, especially
during dinners with her dad. She then asked if I also find C exhausting. I
responded that C has been doing really well and is really trying to find
friends to do things with.  Mum wished C could find a friend who had
similar mental illness she could bond with.

5–7pm had a light dinner

7:15pm Yin yoga class. The class was more meditation-focused and calming
than physically demanding.

9pm meds.

Throughout the day she was analyzing and joking about her diagnosis. She
broke down the word “schizoaffective” and joked that it means she is
“effective.” I asked why she was reframing it that way and she said she
does not want the diagnosis to hold power over her. She is still trying to
challenge her illness and saying it was all circumstantial by outside
events.  Gave her space to process this. I then asked if she had been
seeing her therapist regularly and suggested it could also be something
useful to continue exploring in therapy. She stated that she likes her
therapist.

Today she also showed me a chronological list she has been putting together
of her treatments, hospitalizations, and past medications. She said she is
trying to better understand the timeline of her mental health history and
treatment progression.
( Seems like with a lot of downtime she's focusing on this )

Continued issues with attention. Cannot multitask. She forgot the turkey
bacon on the stove after walking away to get ready. I was in the kitchen so
I was checking on it, but when she returned I asked if she had forgotten
anything and she initially said no. After I reminded her about the stove,
she shrieked and said she needs to put a bigger sign above the oven to
remind herself. She also continues to leave drawers, cupboards, and doors
open after getting items out.

Decided she would rather do a life -drawing class than go to the concert.
So she signed us both to do the drawing class on Sunday instead.
